trips.name: Moss Park
trips.card_teaser: A great county campground with shade and sun, fun amenities, and stunning views of Kennedy Space Center. What better time to decorate a campsite than the holidays?
trips.intro: A great county campground with shade and sun, fun amenities, and stunning views of Kennedy Space Center. What better time to decorate a campsite than the holidays?
body_paragraphs: Many people have expressed a joy in camping over the holidays — when else can you decorate your campsite? But the season can make it hard to get away. Moss Park solves that: a great escape that's still close to home.

Moss Park sits between Lake Mary Jane and Lake Hart, about 20 minutes from Orlando International Airport. Expect shaded, spacious sites, a swimming beach, boat ramps for fishing and paddling, hiking trails, and regular visits from deer and sandhill cranes.

As always, you'll need to book your own site directly through the park. Once you've got your reservation, let us know which site you picked so we can add you to the group camping map — that's how the rest of the group finds you once you're there.
facts: Swim beach, boat ramp, trails, pavilions
trips.note: Reservations go through Orange County Parks & Recreation, not through our group. Book your own site, then share your site number with the group.

trips.name: Wickham Park
trips.card_teaser: A large campground with lots going on — an arts & crafts fair, hiking trails, disc golf, and only 15 minutes from the beach. The hardest part is deciding what to do first.
trips.intro: A large campground with lots going on — an arts & crafts fair, hiking trails, disc golf, and only 15 minutes from the beach. The hardest part is deciding what to do first.
body_paragraphs: Sometimes you want the beach, other times you want a festival — why not both? Wickham Park puts you close to Melbourne's beaches while the park itself keeps you busy with its own art and crafts fair, hiking trails, and disc golf course.
This 391-acre Brevard County park offers full-hookup campsites, fishing lakes, an archery range, and a dog park, all on flat, easy terrain that's simple to navigate with the whole family.
You'll book your own site directly with Brevard County, then let us know which site you picked so we can add you to the group camping map.
facts: Disc golf, archery, fishing lakes, dog park
trips.note: Reservations go through Brevard County Parks & Recreation, not through our group. Book your own site, then share your site number with the group.

trips.name: Jetty Park
trips.eyebrow: Group Trip · May 1, 2026 · Beach Baptism Weekend
trips.card_teaser: Beach Baptism weekend, now a full weekend at the beach with cruise ship views. Air-conditioned cabins are still available — but this one books out fast.
trips.intro: Beach Baptism weekend doesn't have to just be a day. Enjoy a full weekend at the beach with views of the cruise ships. There are even still cabins available, complete with beds and AC!
body_paragraphs: Jetty Park is a wildly popular campground right on the beach in Port Canaveral — a perfect place to enjoy the sand, fish off the pier, surf some waves, and watch the cruise ships head out.
This is our biggest group trip of the year, timed around Beach Baptism weekend. Jetty Park does book out far in advance, so if you want to join us, don't wait too long to reserve your spot.

If you're tent camping, stick to a dome tent — it's highly recommended. The wind off the water gets serious out there, and a few of our group members have lost rigid, square-frame tents that couldn't flex with the gusts.
Book through the link below, then let us know which site or cabin you picked so we can add you to the group camping map.
facts: Plenty of standard sites & A/C cabins
Dome tents only — high winds have taken down rigid frames
trips.note: Reservations go through Jetty Park directly, not through our group. Book your own site or cabin, then share your site or cabin number with the group.

Jetty Park beach with a cruise ship passing in the background

trips.name: Lake Louisa State Park
trips.card_teaser: Trade the ocean for spring-fed lakes at this sprawling state park in the Clermont hills — kayaking, miles of trails, and cabins or glamping tents if you'd rather skip the tent.
trips.intro: Trade the ocean for spring-fed lakes at this sprawling state park in the Clermont hills — kayaking, miles of trails, and cabins or glamping tents if you'd rather skip the tent.
body_paragraphs: Lake Louisa State Park trades salt water for spring-fed lakes — three of them, tucked into the rolling citrus-country hills of Clermont. It's a different kind of camping weekend: quieter water, longer trails, and a campground built for settling in rather than rushing around.

The park sits about 45 minutes southwest of Orlando, between Lake Louisa, Lake Hammond, and Lake Dixie. Expect 60 full-facility sites with 30- and 50-amp hookups, plus cabins and a few glamping-style bell tents if you'd rather skip the tent setup. Bring a kayak or paddleboard — the lakes are made for it — or walk the seven miles of paved road and twenty miles of trail.

As always, you'll need to book your own site directly through Florida State Parks. Once you've got your reservation, let us know which site (or cabin) you picked so we can add you to the group camping map — that's how the rest of the group finds you once you're there.
facts: 3 lakes, kayaking, cabins & glamping, 20 mi of trails
trips.note: Reservations go through Florida State Parks, not through our group. Book your own site, then share your site number with the group.
